The engineer will clean camera datasets, evaluate object-detection models, investigate false positives, and collaborate with controls engineers during field trials. Required qualifications: three years of machine-learning or computer-vision work; Python; dataset preparation; and model evaluation. Preferred qualifications: robotics sensors, ROS, and real-time inference. The hybrid role is based in Kitchener with regular lab days. The employer requires Canadian authorization and offers no sponsorship. The lab keeps a hardware-integration backlog and expects perception engineers to explain trade-offs to controls partners. Controls partners attend perception reviews and contribute requirements for field testing. The position includes experiment reviews with safety engineers and written recommendations for the next robot firmware cycle. The engineer helps maintain test-case inventories and participates in weekly field-readiness reviews. Mossline Automation published this full-time opening on 2026-07-10 and describes it as a mid-level role.

posting.title: Robotics Software Engineer
posting.description: Aster Vale Robotics is hiring a Robotics Software Engineer for its Autonomy Runtime team in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. This hybrid, full-time mid-level role maintains C++ ROS 2 nodes that publish synchronized pose and route-plan updates for warehouse robots. You will integrate lidar and wheel-odometry health checks into the navigation runtime and its incident logs, then run Linux replay sessions and supervised floor trials before each autonomy software release. Production C++ firmware or peripheral-driver experience is not part of this position; the critical qualification is production C++ ROS 2 ownership for an autonomy runtime. Required qualifications are Linux logging and field-debugging experience plus hands-on sensor integration. Simulation-based safety checks and Python diagnostic tooling are preferred. The posting was published on 2026-07-20, and the employer confirms work authorization support for the stated location. Engineers share release notes with operations and take part in a practical floor-trial rotation.
